channel.title: News on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in News on The Guyana Brief

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Wednesday Brief: Guyanese Takes IICA Helm, Census Controversy, and Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: Muhammad Ibrahim becomes IICA Director-General, census data sparks housing debate, cybercrime law faces criticism, and AG slams courts for ignoring legislation.

item.title: Tuesday's Guyana Brief: Apps, Laws, and Courtroom Drama
item.description: Attorney General scolds magistrates for ignoring laws, government promises 87 new apps, and President Ali discovers swamps can become highways (who knew?). Welcome to Tuesday in paradise!

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Monday Brief: Crime Stats, Search & Rescue Upgrade, and Traffic Chaos
item.description: Murders up 11% despite overall crime drop, new $123M search and rescue system launched, over 3,100 traffic offences in one week, and financial system reforms promised.

item.title: Monday's Guyana Brief: The Week Starts With a Yelling
item.description:
Good morning, Guyana! ☕

Welcome to Monday, where last week’s problems are this week’s problems with a fresh coat of optimistic denial.

Nothing got fixed over the weekend. Shocked? You shouldn’t be.

Today’s menu: GuySuCo gets scolded (again), The Mohameds perfect the art of not leaving, crime stats achieve Olympic-level creativity, and we learn why complaining about bribery is somehow worse than actual bribery. Just another week in paradise!

📊 TODAY’S NUMBERS

Mohamed Appeals: 47 (their lawyer’s yacht fund thanks you)

item.title: Sunday's Guyana Brief: The Mohameds Strike Back
item.description:
📊 SUNDAY QUICK STATS

🎭 THE MOHAMEDS PRESENT: “THEY’RE NOT GOING ANYWHERE” (THE MUSICAL)

This week, the Mohamed legal saga reached peak absurdity. Let’s recap the highlights:

This Week’s Timeline:

  • Monday: Acting Chief Justice says “No stay”
  • Monday (5 minutes later): Defense appeals to Full Court
  • Tuesday: Courtroom drama with first witness
  • Thursday: More drama with “late evidence”
  • Friday: Nazar’s op-ed declares “government declared war on his family”

Defense Attorney’s Battle Cry: “The Mohameds are NOT going anywhere.”

item.title: Saturday's Guyana Brief: Weekend News Circus
item.description:
📊 WEEKEND QUICK STATS

🎭 THE MOHAMEDS PLAY THE VICTIM CARD

The Mohameds are back with another performance, and this time they’re playing the victim. According to their lawyer, Sanjeev Datadin, the US wants to extradite them because they’re “Indian Guyanese” and “successful businessmen.”

Let’s Break This Down:

Their Argument: America is targeting them because of their ethnicity and success.

item.title: Friday's Guyana Brief: Storage Unit Edition
item.description:
📦 BREAKING: GUYANA VOLUNTEERS AS AMERICA’S STORAGE UNIT

In the wildest plot twist since the 2020 elections, Guyana is in “productive discussions” with the United States about accepting third-country nationals.

Let That Sink In: America wants to deport people from OTHER countries to Guyana. Not Americans. Not even people who’ve ever BEEN to Guyana. Just random third-country nationals that America doesn’t want.
